title: Code Factory: Proof-by-Sabotage Software Factory
description:
Code Factory is a proof-first, open-source software factory for turning specifications, behavior checks, design contracts, policy verification, and release receipts into auditable delivery. Version 0.20 adds a transactional, hash-linked Product Mission graph with independent creator and validator identities, exact milestone verification, hard budget exhaustion, human pause and plan revision, fresh-context resume, Mermaid export, and an optional LangGraph checkpoint adapter. It also adds secret-free multi-provider BYOK policies with provider/model and IDE allowlists, quality floors, price metadata, mission budget ceilings, routing bias, and cache-continuity hints across CLI, Studio, VS Code, and JetBrains. Provider routing performs no provider call and stores no credential value. The supervised hosted tenant control plane, authenticated pull-request assurance, signed Capability Packs, seven starter targets, Factory Studio, and editor integrations remain included. Public documentation assets include exact Studio captures, the narrated quick start, Mermaid diagrams, and a nine-stage conceptual visual walkthrough; the illustrations are not UI screenshots or measured outcome evidence.

Evidence boundary: the hosted reference is deployable and supervised but does not claim managed HA, automatic disaster recovery, SCIM, SAML enrollment, managed KMS, SOC 2, or an SLA. External runtimes remain responsible for provider credential injection, paid model calls, GitHub publication credentials, and production effects. A passing receipt proves the declared checks and supplied environment, not arbitrary production security.

Dual licensed under MIT or Apache-2.0.
